title: Spring Testing and Testcontainers
learningObjectives: Apply **Spring Testing and Testcontainers** with production-grade tests, tools, and measurable outcomes
Explain trade-offs between test types: unit, slice, integration, contract, e2e
Use profiling and benchmarking vocabulary correctly in senior interviews
Deliver behavioral answers with STAR structure tied to real architecture decisions
Run mock interviews with follow-up depth on testing and performance
Connect reliability practices from earlier phases to capstone scenarios

sections.title: Why Spring Testing and Testcontainers matters
sections.content: **Spring** **testing** is about **choosing** the **right** **slice**. **Testcontainers** bridges **unit** confidence and **prod** **parity** without **manual** **VM** **farms**.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Spring Testing and Testcontainers
sections.content:
### Day 86 — **Spring** **testing** & **Testcontainers**

### 1. Slices
**Web**, **DataJpa**, **Json** tests.

### 2. MockMvc
**Status**, **jsonPath**, **exception** handlers.

### 3. Containers
**Lifecycle**, **reuse**, **CI** **caching**.

### 4. Properties
**Dynamic** **ports** **wiring**.

### 5. Speed
**Parallel** **CI** **jobs** and **reuse** **modes**.

### 6. Story
**404** **test** caught **missing** **@ControllerAdvice** mapping.

sections.title: Basic — Slice test intent
Intermediate — MockMvc check
Advanced — Testcontainers hook
CI with Testcontainers
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 86 — CI
participant Pipeline
participant Docker
Pipeline -> Docker : start deps
Docker --> Pipeline : ports
Pipeline -> Tests : Spring context
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — Spring Testing and Testcontainers (Day 86 assignment)
sections.problem:
Print **exactly** two lines:
1. Implemented: Spring Testing & Testcontainers
2. Focus: @WebMvcTest for 404 unknown IDs.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: **@WebMvcTest** **scope**?
conceptual.answer: Controller layer + web slice; does not load full @SpringBootApplication context by default. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Spring Testing and Testcontainers**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Controller layer + web slice; does not load full @SpringBootApplication context by default. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Controller layer + web slice; does not load full @SpringBootApplication context by default Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

conceptual.question: **@SpringBootTest** vs **slice**?
conceptual.answer: Full integration vs focused layer—speed and failure isolation trade-off. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Spring Testing and Testcontainers**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Full integration vs focused layer—speed and failure isolation trade-off. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Full integration vs focused layer—speed and failure isolation trade-off. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

conceptual.question: **Testcontainers** **value**?
conceptual.answer: Real Postgres/Kafka in disposable containers—catches driver and SQL issues mocks miss. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Spring Testing and Testcontainers**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Real Postgres/Kafka in disposable containers—catches driver and SQL issues mocks miss. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **Spring Testing and Testcontainers** deploy correlates with **5xx** spike.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Freeze risky changes; open **SLO** dashboard.

**(2) Root cause** **Config** regression or **dependency** **saturation**.

**(3) Fix** Rollback or **toggle** **flag**; restore **SLO** then root-cause.

**(4) Prevention** Add **canary** gate and **diff** **alerts** on **golden** metrics. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: How do you **communicate** **impact** and **ETA** during this incident?
followUps.answer: Share **SLO** **burn**, **customer** **regions** affected, **mitigation** steps in flight, and **explicit** unknowns. Avoid **blaming** **users**; focus on **measurable** recovery criteria. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: Which **post-incident** **action** items are **non-negotiable**?
followUps.answer: At minimum: **runbook** update, **dashboard** gap closed, **test** that **reproduces** the failure, and **owner** with **deadline**. Tie themes to **error** **budget** policy. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Annotation | Recall |
|---|---|
| @WebMvcTest | Controller slice |
| @DataJpaTest | JPA slice |
| @SpringBootTest | Full |
| @MockBean | Replace bean |
| @AutoConfigureMockMvc | MVC test |
| TC | Real dependency |
| DynamicPropertySource | Wire ports |
| reuse | Speed CI |
| awaitility | Async assert |
| TestRestTemplate | HTTP blackbox |
